Amylyx Pharmaceuticals, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Amylyx Pharmaceuticals, Inc., a biopharmaceutical company focused on developing therapies for neurodegenerative diseases and endocrine conditions, has released its Form 10-Q report for the second quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational developments, reflecting significant strides in both areas.

Financial Highlights

  • Product revenue, net: $0 million, reflecting the discontinuation of RELYVRIO®/ALBRIOZA™.
  • Loss from operations: $(42.9) million, a 44% improvement from the prior year period due to reduced operating expenses.
  • Net loss: $(41.4) million, a 43% improvement from the prior year period, driven by cost reductions and restructuring efforts.
  • Net loss per share — basic and diluted: $(0.46), reflecting the impact of the net loss on a per-share basis.

Business Highlights

  • Product Development: Amylyx Pharmaceuticals is advancing three investigational therapies: avexitide for PBH, AMX0035 for Wolfram syndrome and PSP, and AMX0114 for ALS. Avexitide is in a pivotal Phase 3 trial, AMX0035 is in Phase 2 trials, and AMX0114 is in a Phase 1 trial.
  • Clinical Trials: The Phase 3 LUCIDITY trial for avexitide in PBH is underway, with completion of recruitment expected in 2025 and topline data anticipated in the first half of 2026. The Phase 2b/3 ORION trial for AMX0035 in PSP has completed enrollment, with interim analysis expected in the third quarter of 2025.
  • Research and Development Focus: The company is focusing on neurodegenerative diseases and endocrine conditions, with significant investment in clinical trials for avexitide and AMX0035. Research and development expenses increased in the second quarter of 2025 compared to the prior year period.
  • Operational Restructuring: A restructuring plan was implemented in 2024, reducing the workforce by approximately 70% to focus resources on key clinical and preclinical programs. This has led to a decrease in selling, general, and administrative expenses.
  • Future Outlook: Amylyx expects to continue generating operating losses for the foreseeable future but anticipates that its cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities will be sufficient to fund operations through 2026. The company plans to finance operations through existing resources and potential future equity or debt offerings.
  • Regulatory Designations: Avexitide has received Breakthrough Therapy Designation for PBH and congenital HI, Rare Pediatric Disease Designation in congenital HI, and Orphan Drug Designation for hyperinsulinemic hypoglycemia.
  • Collaborations: In December 2024, Amylyx announced a collaboration with Gubra A/S to develop a novel long-acting GLP-1 receptor antagonist, with plans to identify a lead development candidate for IND-enabling studies.
  • Market Withdrawal: The company voluntarily discontinued the marketing authorizations for RELYVRIO®/ALBRIOZA™ in the U.S. and Canada based on results from the Phase 3 PHOENIX trial, impacting revenue generation from these products.

SEC Filing: Amylyx Pharmaceuticals, Inc. [ AMLX ] - 10-Q - Aug. 07, 2025

data.title: LENSAR Reports Second Quarter 2025 Results and Provides Business Update
data.description:
LENSAR, Inc., a global medical technology company specializing in advanced robotic laser solutions for cataract treatment, has released its financial results for the quarter ending June 30, 2025. The company also provided updates on key operational initiatives and the progress of its proposed merger with Alcon.

Financial Highlights

For the second quarter of 2025, LENSAR reported total revenue of $13.9 million, marking a 10% increase from $12.6 million in the same period of 2024. This growth was primarily driven by a 23% increase in worldwide procedure volumes. The company's net loss for the quarter was $1.8 million, or ($0.15) per share, a significant improvement from the $9.0 million loss, or ($0.79) per share, reported in the second quarter of 2024. The improvement in net loss was largely due to changes in warrant liability associated with stock price fluctuations.

Business and Operational Highlights

During the quarter, LENSAR placed 18 ALLY Robotic Cataract Laser Systems™, increasing the installed base to approximately 165 systems, which represents a 107% growth compared to the previous year. The total combined installed base of LENSAR Laser Systems and ALLY Systems grew by 23% to approximately 410 units. The company's laser systems performed over 21% of total U.S. procedures in the quarter, up 3% from the same period last year.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

LENSAR's proposed merger with Alcon continues to progress, with the transaction expected to close by the end of the year. The merger was overwhelmingly approved by LENSAR's stockholders, with over 99% of votes cast in favor. The company is currently working with the U.S. Federal Trade Commission to respond to additional information requests.

Management's Perspective

Nick Curtis, President and CEO of LENSAR, commented on the results, stating, "Our second quarter results reflect the continued, strong momentum of ALLY with 18 systems placed in the quarter and an additional 18 systems in backlog. Moreover, we continued to see strong procedure growth, with worldwide procedure volumes increasing 23% over second quarter 2024 levels."

Future Outlook

The company remains optimistic about the future, with expectations to close the merger with Alcon by the end of the year. LENSAR continues to focus on expanding its installed base and increasing procedure volumes, leveraging its advanced robotic technologies and AI-integrated systems.

SEC Filing: LENSAR, Inc. [ LNSR ] - 8-K - Aug. 07, 2025

data.title: Crocs Cash and Cash Equivalents Were $201M as of June 30 >CROX
Becton Dickinson Boosts Outlook as 3Q Results Beat Estimates
data.description:
By Connor Hart

Becton Dickinson raised its outlook after logging higher-than-expected earnings and revenue in its fiscal third quarter, boosted by better sales of its medical devices.

The maker of catheters and other devices on Thursday said it now expects adjusted earnings of $14.30 to $14.45 a share for the full fiscal year, up from a prior outlook of $14.06 to $14.34 a share. The company backed its revenue forecast of $21.8 billion to $21.9 billion.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet are looking for adjusted earnings of $14.17 a share on revenue of $21.82 billion.

Chief Executive Tom Polen said strong margin and per-share earnings growth in the most recent quarter enabled the company to raise its full-year outlook.

Looking forward, Polen noted the company remains focused on completing its previously disclosed deal to divest its biosciences and diagnostic-solutions business to Waters Corp. for around $17.5 billion, while also further accelerating sequential growth.

For its three months ended June 30, the Franklin Lakes, N.J., company posted net income of $574 million, or $2 a share, compared with $487 million, or $1.68 a share, a year earlier.

Adjusted earnings were $3.68 a share, topping the $3.40 that analysts expected.

Revenue climbed 10% to $5.51 billion, just ahead of the $5.49 billion that Wall Street modeled.

Sales across BD Medical jumped 14% to $2.93 billion, while sales across BD Interventional climbed 7.2% to $1.33 billion. These gains were slightly offset by the company's life-sciences business, where sales edged 0.5% lower to $1.25 billion.

Shares rose 5.6%, to $182.49, in premarket trading.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com

data.title: Bank of England cuts rates to 4% after narrow 5-4 vote
data.description:
The Bank of England cut interest rates on Thursday but four of its nine policymakers - worried about high inflation - sought to keep borrowing costs on hold, suggesting the BoE's run of rate cuts might be nearing an end.

Difficulty reaching an agreement meant the Monetary Policy Committee held two votes for the first time in its history.

With the MPC facing the conflicting risks posed by an inflation rate that the BoE forecasts will soon be double its 2% target and a worsening of job losses, Governor Andrew Bailey and four colleagues backed lowering Bank Rate to 4% from 4.25%.

But that was only after a first round of voting ended in a 4-4-1 split with external MPC member Alan Taylor initially backing a half-point cut.

The four members of the MPC who backed keeping rates on hold included Clare Lombardelli, the deputy governor for monetary policy, who broke from the majority for the first time. Chief Economist Huw Pill also voted to keep Bank Rate at 4.25%.

British government bond yields rose and stocks fell after the announcement. Sterling jumped by about half a cent against the U.S. dollar.

"The close vote split and the minutes of the meeting underscore the division on the MPC," Yael Selfin, chief economist at KPMG UK, said. "The division reflects the two-sided risks to the inflation outlook and the uncertainty under which policymakers are operating." She said she expected one final BoE rate cut in November.

The BoE repeated its guidance about "a gradual and careful approach" to further cuts in borrowing costs but added a new line to its message on the outlook.

"The restrictiveness of monetary policy had fallen as Bank Rate had been reduced," it said, no longer directly saying that policy was still restrictive. It repeated that there was no pre-set path for borrowing costs.

A halt to the process of cutting rates would be a blow for finance minister Rachel Reeves and Prime Minister Keir Starmer, who have struggled to meet their promise to voters to speed up Britain's slow economic growth.

Bailey said the decision to cut rates for the fifth time since August last year was "finely balanced" although he thought they were still on a downward path.

"But any future rate cuts will need to be made gradually and carefully," Bailey said in a statement.

CONFLICTING RISKS

The BoE is being pulled in different directions, leaving analysts, as well as its own policymakers, divided on its most likely moves in the coming months.

Britain's jobs market has weakened in recent months in part due to a tax hike by Reeves on employers and U.S. President Donald Trump's trade war.

But inflation is rising. The BoE revised up its forecast for a peak in inflation to 4% in September from 3.7% and said it would remain alert to the risk that the increase in prices - especially those of food - pushes up wage deals and longer-term price pressures.

"Overall, the MPC judges that the upside risks around medium-term inflationary pressures have moved slightly higher since May," the summary of the meeting said.

The BoE said it expected inflation to return to its 2% target only in the second quarter of 2027, three months later than its previous forecast.

By contrast, the European Central Bank expects inflation in the euro zone to hold below 2%. It has cut borrowing costs eight times since June of last year, three more reductions than those of the BoE.

Inflation has been above the BoE's 2% target almost constantly since May 2021.

The British central bank said it expected economic growth of 0.3% in the July-to-September period, up from 0.1% in the second quarter. Its longer-term growth forecasts were little changed from its report in May with annual growth of just over 1% in the coming years.

Before Thursday's expected rate cut, investors were mostly pricing in another rate cut in November but only one or two more in 2026.

Bailey and other top BoE officials were due to hold a press conference at 1130 GMT.

data.title: Ukraine steel output down 7% so far in 2025, union says
data.description:
Ukraine's steel output fell 7% in the first seven months of 2025 to 4.26 million metric tons, data from the Ukrainian steel producers' union showed on Thursday.

Steel production has suffered since Russia invaded the country in February 2022, with two large steel plants destroyed during the subsequent conflict and now located in occupied territory.

Ukraine, formerly a leading steel producer and exporter, reported a 70.7% drop in output in 2022 to 6.3 million tons. It fell to 6 million tons in 2023 but increased to 7.58 million tons last year.

data.title: Securities Fraud Class Action Filed Against Neogen Corporation (NEOG) - Levi & Korsinsky Reminds Investors of September 16, 2025
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / August 7, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your Neogen Corporation investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/neogen-corporation-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=160174&wire=1&utm_campaign=27

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against Neogen Corporation that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between January 5, 2023 and June 3, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: According to the filed complaint, defendants issued a series of materially false and misleading statements which led investors to believe that the integration with the Food Safety Division of the 3M Company was progressing much better than it actually was. In addition, even when the Company was forced to reveal that certain "inefficiencies" arose as a result of the integration, defendants downplayed them and assured investors that they were fully aware and committed to resolving them quickly.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in Neogen Corporation stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/neogen-corporation-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=160174&wire=1&utm_campaign=27 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.
